excerpts:

APA PsycTests: Citation and Description

## Controlled Oral Word Association Test

Benton, A. L., Hamsher, d. S. K., & Sivan, A. B. (1983). _Controlled Oral Word Association Test (COWAT)_ [Database record]. APA PsycTests.

https://doi.org/10.1037/t10132-000

* * *

### Description

The Controlled Oral Word Association Test (COWAT; Benton, Hamsher & Sivan; 1983) is a verbal fluency test that measures spontaneous production of words belonging to the same category or beginning with some designated letter. There are two commonly used forms of the test, one using the letters F, A, and S, and the other using C, F, and L.
The participant is usually asked to name words beginning with a letter, excluding proper nouns, for one minute; this procedure is repeated three times. Examinees are allowed 60 seconds for each trial. The examiner records the words provided by the participant. The whole examination usually takes 5–10 minutes. performance on the COWAT is measured by calculating the total number of acceptable words produced for all three letters. Errors and perseverations (word repetitions) are not included in this score. The COWAT is a subtest of the Multilingual Aphasia Examination (MAE; Benton, Hamsher, & Sivan, 1994). (APA PsycTests Database Record (c) 2019 APA, all rights reserved)

## Privacy Preference Center

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. results.title: Microwaves and behavior. - APA PsycNet
excerpts: Discusses the study of radio-frequency electromagnetic fields, especially microwaves, in psychology, noting the involvement of Soviet scientists in this area.

## Resolve a DOI Name

Type or paste a known DOI name exactly—including its prefix and suffix—into the text box below and then
‘submit’ to resolve it.

DOIs include a prefix (prefixes always start with `10.` ) and a suffix, separated by a forward
slash ( `/` ). Prefacing the DOI with `doi.org/` will turn it into an actionable
link, for example, <https://doi.org/10.3897/rio.7.e67379> .
Clicking that link will ‘resolve’ it, i.e. redirect to the latest information about the object it
identifies, even if the object changes or moves.

Send questions or comments to info@doi.org .

results.title: American Psychologist
excerpts: Each issue of American Psychologist will highlight one article by selecting it as an “ Editor’s Choice ” paper. The chosen manuscript will be included in a newsletter sent to APA members and nonmembers alike.

results.title: The Effects of Microwave Exposure on Thymidine-3H Uptake
excerpts: To approximate the microwave dose as conventionally reported in terms mW/cm2, the following methods were employed. A polystyrene jar was filled 200 g of tap water, the surface area of the water calculated, and the jar placed the exposure cavity and water temperature measured.
